_meta.note: Daemon-backed bounded PTY stdin write. The request field is bytes; writes are denied while a secret prompt is active so LLM-supplied secrets are not forwarded. TB-7: job_id carries an opaque-id format note (copy verbatim, e.g. job_<32hex>; from pty_command_start) so the first call uses the right id. US5/FR-041: an optional bounded wait_ms (with optional cursor, default 0) runs a settle-window read over the PTY job bucket AFTER the write and returns the combed signals the write provoked in the SAME response (cursor_in, next_cursor, has_more, dropped_count, events) -- the same shape family as shell_session_exec. The secret-prompt denial fires BEFORE the write and is unchanged by wait_ms. Omit wait_ms for today's immediate return, which serializes byte-identically (the combed fields are absent).

unsupported_platform_error_example.message: daemon ipc error [UnsupportedPlatform]: PTY command runtime is not available on this platform yet (ConPTY support pending)

secret_input_denied_error_example.message: daemon ipc error [SecretInputDenied]: secret prompt active; LLM-supplied input denied

invariants: job_id and bytes are required; cursor and wait_ms are optional (US5/FR-041)
bytes is a UTF-8 string capped at 4096 bytes by the daemon
without wait_ms the response reports bytes_written and post-write secret_prompt_active only, byte-identical to the pre-US5 shape
with wait_ms the response adds the combed batch cursor_in/next_cursor/has_more/dropped_count/events (same shape family as shell_session_exec)
secret prompts produce a typed SecretInputDenied error BEFORE the write, unchanged by wait_ms; the bytes are not written and no settle read runs
success payload never contains echoed stdin, PTY output, or screen content; events are structured signals only
